page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 44 vCPUs mapped to 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 176 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor architecture is x86_64, incorporating a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the c3-standard-44 in the average performance tier for most standard compute tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1719.0 and a multi-core score of 15718.0. However, it demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 41.93 GB/sec and a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 38725.32, both ranking in the top 25 percent. Image processing is also a relative strength, scoring 16162.0 in Geekbench. For language model tasks, the instance handles prompt processing for medium 7B models at 187.15 tokens/sec and text generation for small 135M models at 285.08 tokens/sec, though performance drops to 2.39 tokens/sec for large 70B model text generation due to the lack of hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-44 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) enhances its capability for vector and matrix operations. This makes the instance well-suited for web serving, software compilation, medium-scale database operations, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that do not justify dedicated GPU resources.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 44 vCPUs, 22 physical cores, and 176 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on dedicated CPU allocation and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate average overall performance, but show strong capabilities in static web serving (41.93 GB/sec), CPU encryption, and image processing. It is also capable of moderate LLM inference, processing medium 7B model prompts at 187.15 tokens/sec. This instance is best suited for balanced enterprise workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 is a general-purpose server featuring 44 vCPUs, 176 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It offers strong performance in web serving and encryption.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 is an x86_64 server with 44 vCPUs, 176 GB RAM, and a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, optimized for web serving, encryption, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 44 vCPUs and 176 GB RAM powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor
Dedicated CPU allocation with advanced AVX-512 and Intel AMX instruction sets
Strong performance in static web serving, CPU encryption, and image processing
Capable of moderate LLM inference for small and medium-sized models
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads
